Plate code and batch number required. Introduction This document is intended to describe the quality of the Thermo Scientific™ Sensititre™ System manufacture and the Sensititre method evaluation and performance against the broth microdilution reference method. Manufacturing site quality statement The Thermo Scientific Sensititre antimicrobial susceptibility and identification plates are manufactured by TREK Diagnostic Systems (Part of Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc) in East Grinsted, UK. The manufacturing site follows the Quality Management System for medical devices – ISO 13485:2016 and is certified with BSI. Manufacturing site is also part of Medical Device Single Audit Program for all 5 regions (USA, Canada, Japan, Australia, Brazil) – and certified with LRQA. Quality Control All Sensititre susceptibility and identification plates undergo extensive quality control performance testing prior to product release. All plate formats undergo the same intensity of testing regardless if they are to be used as Research Use Only, Veterinary Use or In-vitro Diagnostic Use; this ensures product integrity. Quality control testing of all susceptibility plates is assessed with wide variety of organisms, primarily with four CLSI & EUCAST approved micro-organisms; Staphylococcus aureus ATCC™ 29213™, Enterococcus faecalis ATCC™ 29212™, Escherichia coli ATCC™ 25922™ and Pseudomonas aeruginosa ATCC™ 27853™. All antimicrobials within the plate must achieve a set of five, on-scale, minimum inhibitory concentrations within the quality control ranges cited by CLSI/EUCAST or Thermo Fisher Scientific in-house Sensititre development ranges for each micro-organism tested. Only when the set criteria have been met can the product be released for sale. Additional micro-organisms are routinely used such as; Klebsiella pneumoniae ATCC™ 700603™ and Escherichia coli ATCC™ 35218™ to quality control assess the more complex combination antimicrobics. Additional quality control testing methodology, such as radial diffusion assays, are also used to determine the potency of the antimicrobic within a well. EU SURV CID10917 www.thermoscientific.com/contactus Methods The reference method for the majority of antimicrobial susceptibility testing applications is broth microdilution. Sensititre method has been evaluated against a broth microdilution reference method, such as ISO 20776-1:2020 and NCCLS (CLSI) M07-A6, as further described below. The evaluation of the Sensititre method has been performed as follows: the Sensititre 96-well plates, containing dried antimicrobials in the wells, have been set up for bacterial isolate testing - with relevant inoculum strength, growth medium, sample volume and incubation conditions - according to the instructions on the Sensititre plate technical insert. Details of the method set up is described on Tables 1 and 2 below under ‘Sensititre’ column. The broth microdilution reference method 96-well plates, with liquid antimicrobial suspensions in the wells, have been prepared according to of the instructions of a reference method standard (eg. ISO 20776-1:2020). Details on the ISO 20776-1:2020 broth microdilution reference method (for rapidly growing aerobic bacteria) and NCCLS (CLSI) M07-A6 broth microdilution reference method (for Campylobacter spp.) plate set up for bacterial isolate testing – with relevant inoculum strength, growth medium, sample volume and incubation conditions – are detailed on Tables 1 and 2 below under the respective columns. The isolates used in the evaluation studies have been isolated from clinical samples. During the evaluation studies quality control samples (isolates with known minimum inhibitory concentration ranges, sourced from well-established strain collections, such as ATCC) have been tested daily. Result agreement with Campylobacter spp. Campylobacter spp. Antimicrobial agent Number of isolates tested Essential Agreement ERYTHROMYCIN 160 93.0% CHLORAMPHENICOL 307 96.0% CIPROFLOXACIN 160 94.0% GENTAMICIN 160 98.0% TETRACYCLINE 160 97.0% ERTAPENEM No data available EU SURV CID10917 www.thermoscientific.com/contactus Conclusions The performance of the Sensititre method has been evaluated against the broth microdilution reference method, such as ISO 20776-1:2020 and NCCLS (CLSI) M07-A6, by comparing MIC results from a number of clinical bacterial isolates tested with both methods. The acceptance criteria on accuracy of a test device, as stipulated by ISO 20776-2:2007, is that the test device should have an overall essential agreement ≥90% of results when compared to the reference broth microdilution results. The evaluation data show an essential agreement of ≥90% with all the antimicrobial agents, where data is available. No data is available to show the agreement of Sensititre method to the reference method with Ertapenem and Campylobacter spp. Ertapenem has not been tested on Campylobacter spp. isolates as Ertapenem is not a recommended antimicrobial agent for treatment of clinical Campylobacter spp. infections. The results shown support that the performance of the Sensititre method is in good agreement with the broth microdilution reference method.
